cases.question: Show the month-over-month growth rate of gross merchandise value.
cases.sql:
WITH monthly_gmv AS (
  SELECT strftime(o.order_purchase_timestamp, '%Y-%m') AS month,
         SUM(i.price) AS gmv
  FROM olist_order_items i
  JOIN olist_orders o ON i.order_id = o.order_id
  WHERE i.price IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT month,
       gmv,
       CASE
         WHEN LAG(gmv) OVER (ORDER BY month) IS NOT NULL
          AND LAG(gmv) OVER (ORDER BY month) <> 0
         THEN (gmv - LAG(gmv) OVER (ORDER BY month)) / LAG(gmv) OVER (ORDER BY month) * 100
         ELSE NULL
       END AS mom_growth_rate
FROM monthly_gmv
ORDER BY month;
cases.narrative: GMV month-over-month growth was extraordinarily volatile in the early period—driven by very small base values—before stabilizing into a more moderate, oscillating pattern from mid-2017 onward. The standout peak occurred in November 2017, when GMV reached 1,010,271.37 on 52.10% MoM growth, after which growth alternated between modest gains and declines through mid-2018. The series ends with a near-total collapse in September 2018, where GMV fell to 145.00, a -99.98% drop from the prior month's 854,686.33.

cases.question: Show the cumulative gross merchandise value by month as a running total.

WITH monthly AS (
  SELECT strftime(o.order_purchase_timestamp, '%Y-%m') AS purchase_month,
         SUM(i.price) AS gmv

cases.question: For each customer state, what is the top product category by revenue?
cases.sql:
WITH state_category_revenue AS (
  SELECT
    c.customer_state,
    COALESCE(t.product_category_name_english, p.product_category_name) AS category_english,
    SUM(i.price) AS total_revenue
  FROM olist_customers c
  JOIN olist_orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
  JOIN olist_order_items i ON o.order_id = i.order_id
  JOIN olist_products p ON i.product_id = p.product_id
  LEFT JOIN olist_category_translation t ON p.product_category_name = t.product_category_name
  WHERE i.price IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY c.customer_state, COALESCE(t.product_category_name_english, p.product_category_name)
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT
    customer_state,
    category_english,
    total_revenue,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_state ORDER BY total_revenue DESC) AS rn
  FROM state_category_revenue
)
SELECT
  customer_state,
  category_english AS top_category,
  total_revenue
FROM ranked
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY customer_state;
